zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Furnival於1250年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾沃可索普時，她的父親Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire21歲，母親Maud FitzJohn14歲。她於大約1272年與Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath在英國英格蘭越可爾雪菲爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1326年在英國素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Furnival于1250年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾沃可索普时，她的父亲Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire21岁，母亲Maud FitzJohn14岁。她于大约1272年与Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath在英国英格兰越可爾雪菲爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1326年在英国素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Furnival
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: locative name from Fourneville (Calvados), Fournival in Saint-Just-en-Chaussée (Oise), or Fournival in Saint-Julien-sur-Sarthe (Orne).

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Furnival was born in 1250, in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire, was 21 and her mother, Maud FitzJohn, was 14. She married Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath about 1272, in Sheffield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1326, in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Furnival est née en 1250, à Worksop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud FitzJohn, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath environ 1272, à Sheffield, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1326, à Stradbroke, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Furnival è nata il nel 1250 nel Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Maud FitzJohn, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath circa 1272, nel Sheffield, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1326, nel Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Furnivalが1250年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウオルクソップで生まれた時，父Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshireは21歳，母Maud FitzJohnは14歳であった。約1272年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアシェフィールドでSir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeathと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1326年にイギリススフォルクストラドブロクで76歳で死去した。

ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Furnival(이)가 1250년 영국 노틴그함실르 워크소프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud FitzJohn(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 대략 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 셰필드에서 Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1326년 영국 수폴크 스트라드브로크에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath aproximadamente 1272, em Sheffield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Furnival родилась в 1250 г. в местности ворксоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Furnival Lord of Hallamshire было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud FitzJohn, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath около 1272 г. в местности шеффиелд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1326 г. в местности страдброк, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.

Se casó con Sir John de Verdun II - Baron of Westmeath aproximadamente 1272, en Sheffield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
